title: RNA isolation
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"e1v1f","text":"This protocol was used in the above Nature Immunology paper","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":36,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"s0r7t","text":"Trizol (GibcoRL, 10296)\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"295b3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8j5ed","text":"100% Isopropanol (Fisher Scientific, A416-500)\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"0voci","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"yzju8","text":"70% Ethanol (Sigma E702-3)\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"unyer","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hjmsd","text":"DepC Treated water (Ambion 9920)\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"xffyf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"pfkts","text":"Chloroform (Fisher Scientific C298-500)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Pellet cells and resuspend in 1 mL of Trizol (I have difficulty isolating quality RNA from less than 1 X 10 6 primary T cells). 1 mL of Trizol can be used for 1 X 10 6 to 10 X 10 6 . It is best to brush the pelleted cells along a rack to dislodge them from the bottom of the tube. Sometimes aggregates will still be visible. Do not resuspend.
Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes
Add 200μL of Chloroform using a barrier pipette tip and shake each sample vigourously
Incubate at room temperature for 3 minutes
Transfer upper aqueous phase into a new RNAase free tube (approximately 0.8-1 mL of volume)
Add 0.5 mL of isopropanol/mL of Trizol used and shake vigourously
Incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes
Pour off supernatant and wash pellet in with 1 mL of 75% ethanol (do not resuspend pellet rather squirt in ethanol so the pellet becomes dislodged from the bottom of tube)
Centrifuge at 12000g for 5 minutes at room temperature
Pour off supernatant and briefly dry pellet (can use a 10μL pipette or a kimwipe corner to get rid of residual ethanol)
Dissolve pellet in 30μL of DepC treated water and place at 55°C for 5-10 minutes.
Spec and take 2μg for cDNA conversion. Store the rest of the RNA at -80°C.

<a href="https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/ni1422 ">Scaffold protein Dlgh1 coordinates alternative p38 kinase activation, directing T cell receptor signals toward NFAT but not NFkappa-B transcription factors by Round, J.L. et al. published in Nature Immunology on 25 Jan, 2007 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/ni1422
